refactor: modernize storage interface types (#1239)

* refactor: modernize storage interface types (list→Sequence, dict→Mapping)

types where mutation is needed (MutableMapping). Convert async def methods
returning iterators to def returning AsyncIterator. Add delete_history_set
to the storage interface.

* refactor: propagate AsyncIterator through semantic memory module

Adapt all callers of storage methods that now return AsyncIterator:
- SemanticService propagates AsyncIterator for search, get_set_features,
  list_set_id_starts_with
- SemanticSessionManager propagates AsyncIterator to the boundary
- MemMachine collects AsyncIterator into lists at the API boundary
- IngestionService collects internally where lists are needed
- Add merge_async_iterators utility for parallel iterator merging
- Update test files to collect from AsyncIterator

- Fix ruff import sorting in semantic_memory.py and test_background

  manager where config_store hasn't been updated yet, convert at
  call sites instead

The router constructs response models with concrete list/dict fields
but the widened model types now expose Sequence/Mapping. Convert at
the serialization boundary.

commit.message:
refactor: nested group callbacks include ancestor context

Nested group callbacks now receive parent data as additional parameters,
giving consumers full context about which parent each child belongs to.

For example, a nested 'acceleration' group inside 'performanceFigures' now
generates: Action<PerformanceFiguresData, AccelerationData> instead of
Action<AccelerationData>.

The callback invocation passes ancestor data variables:
callbackAcceleration(data, nestedData) instead of callbackAcceleration(nestedData).

This pattern scales to any nesting depth since the schema structure is known
at compile time.

commit.message:
Replace annoying procedural ambient audio with musical pad synthesis + file support

- Remove 8 noise-based ambient generators (410 lines of filtered white noise,
  raw oscillator drones, and harsh sound patterns)
- Add warm musical pad synthesis: detuned oscillator chords through lowpass
  filters with slow LFO modulation and subtle feedback-delay reverb
- Each of the 8 categories maps to a distinct chord (nature=C major, ocean=F,
  space=open fifths, peaceful=G major, mysterious=Bb minor, dramatic=A minor,
  adventure=E major, celebration=D major)
- Add brownian (red) noise for smooth texture (nature/ocean/dramatic) instead
  of harsh white noise
- Add file-based audio playback: checks /audio/ambient/{category}.mp3 first,
  falls back to synthesis when files aren't present
- AudioBuffer cache with negative caching (failed loads aren't retried)
- Race condition guard: if category changes during async file load, stale
  result is discarded
- startAmbient() is now async (fire-and-forget from callers, no API change)
- public/audio/ambient/README.md documents expected filenames, format, and
  CC0 sources (Pixabay, Freesound, OpenGameArt, FMA)
- Net reduction: 1079 → 866 lines (-20%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: increase security-guard max-turns from 15 to 25 (#1856)

several turns handling oversized MCP payloads before falling back to
alternative strategies, leaving insufficient turns to complete the review.

Increasing to 25 turns gives adequate headroom for large diffs.

Fix PE task assignment race condition: workers use leader assignments only

Root cause: Each PE instance has its own EngineeringTaskIssueManager with a
separate in-memory cache. Worker PEs independently called FindNextAssignableTask()
on their stale caches, grabbing the same tasks the leader had already assigned or
was working on.

Fix:
- Worker PEs now check AssignmentQueue (from IssueAssignmentMessage) first
- Workers only work on tasks explicitly assigned by the leader, never independently
- If no message pending, workers refresh from GitHub and look for tasks assigned to
  their DisplayName (recovery path)
- Leader PE refreshes cache from GitHub before picking tasks to see worker claims
- Added claim validation: verify task isn't already in-progress by another PE
- Added FindAssignedTo() to EngineeringTaskIssueManager for worker task discovery

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(training)!: make LR scheduler configurable through Hydra and clean up optimization config (#986) fx

# Summary

The LR scheduler, optimizer, and learning rate were previously scattered
across every training config preset as duplicated inline blocks, with
the scheduler hardcoded to `CosineLRScheduler` in
`configure_optimizers`. This PR consolidates all optimization
configuration into a dedicated `optimization` config group, makes both
the optimizer and scheduler fully configurable via the standard Hydra
`_target_` pattern, and cleans up the code paths that read these
settings.

# Changes

**`training/config/training/optimization/default.yaml`** (new)
- Single source of truth for `lr`, `optimizer`, `lr_scheduler`, and
`pl_lr_scheduler`
- `lr_scheduler` uses `_target_: timm.scheduler.CosineLRScheduler` —
preserves existing default behaviour
- `pl_lr_scheduler` controls Lightning wrapper settings (interval,
monitor, etc.) independently of the scheduler itself; defaults to
`interval: step`

**`training/config/training/optimization/optimizer/`** (new)
- `adamw.yaml`, `ademamix.yaml`, `zero.yaml` — ready-made optimizer
presets to drop in via override


**`training/config/training/{default,autoencoder,ensemble,lam,multi,stretched}.yaml`**
- Adds `- optimization: default` to defaults list
- Removes duplicated inline `optimizer:` and `lr:` blocks from all
preset configs

**`training/config/training/diffusion.yaml`**
- Adds `- optimization: default`; overrides only
`optimization.optimizer` inline (custom `weight_decay`/`eps`); inherits
`lr`, `lr_scheduler`, and `pl_lr_scheduler` from the default

**`training/train/tasks/base.py`**
- `configure_optimizers` now instantiates both optimizer and scheduler
via `hydra.utils.instantiate`; if `lr_scheduler` is absent or null,
returns the optimizer alone (constant LR)
- Removed private `_create_optimizer_from_config` and
`_create_scheduler` methods
- `lr_scheduler_step` generalised to handle both timm-style schedulers
(`step_update`) and standard PyTorch schedulers
- `self.lr` renamed to `self.effective_lr`; reads from
`config.training.optimization.lr`

**`training/train/tasks/ensforecaster.py`**
- Same `self.lr` → `self.effective_lr` / `config.training.lr.rate` →
`config.training.optimization.lr` update

**`training/train/train.py`**
- `config.training.lr.rate` / `config.training.lr.iterations` →
`config.training.optimization.lr` / `config.training.max_steps`

**`training/schemas/training.py`**
- Removes `LR` class; adds `OptimizationSchema` grouping `lr`,
`optimizer`, `lr_scheduler`, `pl_lr_scheduler`;
`BaseTrainingSchema.optimization` replaces the old `lr` + `optimizer`
fields

**`training/tests/unit/train/test_optimizer.py`**
- Tests rewritten against the `configure_optimizers` public API; private
helpers no longer tested directly

**`training/tests/integration/config/testing_modifications.yaml`**
- `training.lr.{warmup,rate}` → `training.optimization.lr`

# Usage

Disable scheduling (constant LR):
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    lr_scheduler: null
```

Switch to a different scheduler:
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    lr_scheduler:
      _target_: torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau
      mode: min
      factor: 0.5
      patience: 5
    pl_lr_scheduler:
      monitor: val_loss
      interval: epoch
```

Switch to AdEMAMix optimizer:
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    optimizer:
      _target_: anemoi.training.optimizers.AdEMAMix.AdEMAMix
      betas: [0.9, 0.95, 0.9999]
      alpha: 8.0
```
or use the preset file: `defaults: [optimization/optimizer: ademamix]`

commit.message:
[wasm][coreclr] Implement webcil V1 support in WebcilConverter and readers (#126709)

PR #126388 bumped the shared webcil version from 0 to 1, we need to
update the rest of webcil ecosystem

## Changes

- **Webcil.cs**: Add `TableBase` field to managed `WebcilHeader` struct
(32 bytes for V1)
- **WebcilConverter.cs**: Parameterize version (default V0), write V0
(28-byte) or V1 (32-byte) header based on `webcilVersion` parameter. Add
version validation and WriteStructure bounds checks.
- **WebcilReader.cs**: Read V0 header first (28 bytes), then
conditionally read extra 4-byte TableBase for V1. Version-aware
`SectionDirectoryOffset`.
- **webcil-loader.c** (Mono): Accept both V0 and V1 headers with proper
bounds checking, use correct header size for section offset calculation.

The WebcilConverter defaults to V0, which is what non-R2R builds use.
The R2R/crossgen2 path (WasmObjectWriter + WebcilEncoder) produces V1
independently. Both CoreCLR and Mono decoders now handle V0 and V1.

## Testing

- CoreCLR browser-wasm library tests: 474/476 passed (2 skipped, 0
failed)
- Mono browser-wasm library tests: 469/476 passed (5 pre-existing
DateTime failures, 0 webcil-related)

Supersedes #126698

Vision Read API response and pass them to the LLM cleanup step as
annotated layout metadata.

  [LOW-CONF] computed from spatial relationships

remove headers/footers by position, detect titles by font size, insert
paragraph breaks at gaps/indents, and discard low-confidence noise.

Cache version-resilient hash code on MethodTableAuxiliaryData to fix superlinear behavior with polymorphic recursion (#126534)

`GetVersionResilientTypeHashCode` recomputes hash codes recursively
without memoization. With polymorphic recursion, each level doubles the
number of unique generic instantiations, causing exponential blowup —
`n=31` took ~15s before this fix.

On 64-bit release builds, `MethodTableAuxiliaryData` already had 4 bytes
of implicit alignment padding between the union and `m_pLoaderModule`.

feat(frontend): add tabbed UI and Playwright e2e tests (#20)

## Summary

Fixes the blank frontend (Svelte 5 mount API) and refactors the UI with
tabs. Adds Playwright e2e test suite covering all GUI requirements.

## Changes

### Bug fix
- Replace `new App()` (Svelte 4) with `mount()` (Svelte 5) in `main.ts`
— this was causing the blank page

### Frontend refactor
- Single card with **Create / Edit / Delete / Test redirect** tabs —
only active tab is visible
- Improved `style.css`: tab bar, danger-styled Delete button, result-row
layout

### Playwright e2e tests (26 tests, all passing)
| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `navigation.spec.ts` | Tab switching, mutual exclusivity |
| `create-link.spec.ts` | Fields, redirect codes 301/302/307/308,
success + 400/409 errors |
| `edit-link.spec.ts` | Partial update options, token validation,
401/409 errors |
| `delete-link.spec.ts` | Danger styling, success + 401/404 errors |
| `test-redirect.spec.ts` | Active/inactive links, 404 unknown ID |

All tests mock the API via `page.route()` so they run without a live
backend.

## Running tests
```bash
bash run-ui-tests.sh
```
Requires the frontend running at `http://localhost:5173` (`docker
compose up` or `npm run dev`).

docs: demand chain diagnosis, rescue carb inference, and glycogen pool proxy (EXP-1621–1628)

EXP-1621: Demand chain diagnosis — traces ×5.0 time-integration bug
  in supply-demand model. demand/expected ratio = 0.47× (variable).

EXP-1622: Steady-state balance — β-correction fixes fasting R² but
  catastrophically worsens other contexts (meal R² → -16.6).

EXP-1623: Rescue carb inference — median 16g unannounced (n=1,605),
  52% exceed standard 15g dose, 9% meal-sized (>40g). Severe hypos
  get 29% more rescue carbs than mild.

EXP-1624: Per-patient β fails cross-context (confirms EXP-1614).

EXP-1625: Rescue absorption peaks at 10min post-nadir (unannounced),
  20min (announced). 63 mg/dL total effect in first hour.

EXP-1626: Glycogen pool proxy from observable signals. ANOVA F=1207
  (glycogen bins produce significantly different residuals).

EXP-1627: Perfect monotonic trend (Spearman r=1.000) — effective β
  doubles from depleted (0.27) to overflowing (0.72) glycogen.
  10/11 patients agree. Confound warning: proxy correlates with
  insulin delivery state.

EXP-1628: Pre-hypo glycogen predicts recovery — full glycogen →
  43% larger rebound (105 vs 73 mg/dL), r=0.182, p=10⁻¹⁶.
